turnaround_reason: ACV Auctions was crushed alongside the used-car cycle bust and broader unprofitable-growth SaaS sell-off. Revenue is reaccelerating (+11.8% YoY), insiders have been net buyers (2.45M shares net), and the operating cash flow has turned positive.
bullish_points: Net insider purchases of 2.45M shares (19 buys vs 3 sells) — strong cluster buying
CEO Chamoun made open-market buy at $5.05 in March — bought near the lows
Revenue growing 11.8% YoY with positive operating cash flow ($88M)
$341M cash vs $246M debt — net cash position
30-day momentum +23%, suggesting basing is resolving up
12 analysts cover with mean target $9.34 (+47% upside)
bearish_points: Still losing money: profit margin -8% TTM, operating margin -4.5%
Forward P/E 19x not screamingly cheap given growth profile
Used-car wholesale cycle remains pressured by elevated rates and OEM glut
Still 16% below the 200-day MA — basing thesis dependent on cycle turn
Most recent insider 'grants' are stock awards, not open-market buys
catalyst: Q2 earnings showing continued GMV growth and progression toward sustained profitability
suggested_trade: Spot position; or Jan 2027 $7 calls for leveraged exposure if used-car cycle turns
key_risk: Used-car wholesale volumes decline further or take rate compresses on competitive pricing
would_skip_if: Q2 revenue growth decelerates below 8%; cash flow turns negative again; insider buying stops
